source: Engineering defaults set by theory, NOT fit to any validation corpus (When-in-Rome is CC BY-NC-SA; SWD is CC BY 3.0 and may be scored against, but a threshold fit to its accuracy is still the methodological over-fit the response-6/-7 caveat fences — one composer/one cycle). min_modulation_beats is the phrase-length floor below which a diatonically-related foreign-key span is heard as a tonicization within the parent key rather than a structural modulation: 8 beats = 2 bars in 4/4 sits in the gap between the longest typical tonicization (~1 bar, a single applied-dominant gesture) and the shortest genuine key establishment (a phrase, ~4 bars), and is >= the window/hop boundary grid so the comparison is meaningful at the engine's resolution. min_area_beats floors a structural modulation area (reserved for a future merge-back pass; dormant in slice 1). require_return gates whether a sustained related span must resolve home to count as prolongational (reserved; false selects the conservative brief-or-returns rule). frame_anchor_bonus weights the opening + closing regions when anchor_method='frame_weighted' is selected: the structural frame is the place least likely to be a tonicization, so it is the most tonicization-robust signal for the home key precisely when the interior is full of tonicizations (the A6 brief-7 failure mode, where a repeatedly-tonicized dominant out-totals the tonic by duration). 1.0 = 'frames count double their own duration' — a theory-set assertion weight, not corpus-tuned; on the diagnosed case it recovers the home key without overturning any genuine duration majority (robust across 1..3). anchor_method defaults to 'most_prevalent_region' (the slice-1 behaviour, unchanged); 'frame_weighted' is opt-in pending full-corpus validation.
